title: Stock Market Today Live, July 21: Sensex drops 250 pts, Nifty below 24,200; HDFC Bank lead losers - The HinduBusinessLine
text:
Sensex Today, Nifty 50 | Stock Market Live Updates - Find here all the live updates related to Sensex, Nifty, BSE, NSE, share prices and Indian stock markets for 21st May 2026.

Benchmark equity indices opened on a cautious note on Tuesday morning, with the Sensex and Nifty50 trading marginally in the green after Monday’s losses, as investors weighed mixed global cues and continued to track first-quarter corporate earnings.

Also Read:Crude oil futures trade lower on possible ceasefire reports

The heaviest stock on the benchmarks, HDFC Bank fell 1.1% after losing 5.1% ‌on Monday in its biggest drop in four months.

Six of ‌the 16 major sectors logged losses. The broader small-caps rose 0.2% while mid-caps traded flat.

Broader Asian markets fell, tracking overnight losses on Wall ‌Street as Brent hovered around $90 a barrel.

Monday’s session saw Foreign Institutional Investors (FIIs) remain net sellers at ₹1,305 crore, though Domestic Institutional Investors (DIIs) absorbed much of that pressure with net purchases of ₹1,409 crore, providing a stabilising floor to the market.

Pace Digitek announced that its subsidiary, Lineage Power Private Limited (LPPL), has established the Pace-Lineage Research Center in Pune.

Crompton Greaves Consumer Electricals informed the appointment of Mr. Anuj Lall as Head Integrated Supply Chain with effect from July 21, 2026.

Sensex slipped 251.04 points (-0.32%) to 77,457.48, while Nifty 50 dropped 65.95 points (-0.27%) to 24,172.55

Lupin licenses two cancer drug programs to Kaveri Therapeutics for an $1.6 million stake, enhancing its oncology portfolio.

The company posted consolidated revenue of ₹617 million for Q1 FY27, up 53 per cent from ₹402 million in the same quarter last year

WANBURY shares were down 3% to Rs 329.20 on the NSE, hitting a low of Rs 314.05. USFDA had issued a Form 483 with six observations, following inspection at a facility in Tanuku, West Godavari District, Andhra Pradesh

CDSL shares down 1% to Rs 1,366.70 on the NSE. SEBI imposed a penalty of Rs 1 crore on CDSL for cybersecurity lapses linked to the malware attack that disrupted its systems in November 2022.

Retail health premiums surge 33%; Motor insurance also gains pace with 14% growth; fire insurance continues to weigh on industry performance, with premiums declining 23%

Choice International subsidiaries Choice Consultancy Services Private Limited and Ayoleeza Consultants Private Limited has won/awarded government mandates with an aggregate value of approximately Rs. 191.38 Crores.

Top Nifty Smallcap movers: KVB (+10.83%), Manappuram Finance (+3.68%), CUB (+3.41%), IIFL (+2.86%)

Losers: PGEL (-1.92%), Urban Company (-1.82%), Zensar Tech (-1.77%), Amber (-1.68%)

The institutional investment activity is expected to be driven by strong macroeconomic fundamentals and infrastructure-led growth

Top Nifty Midcap 100 gainers: IndusInd Bank (+2.38%), UPL (+1.97%), AB Capital (+1.94%), Info edge (+1.80%)

Top losers: Dixon (-2.36%), Supreme Industries (-2.23%), Paytm (-1.75%), Premier Energies (-1.63%)

Denta Water and Infra Solutions received LOA from Karnataka Neeravari Nigam Limited for a contract valued at Rs 20.88 crore; and a sub-contract from JNS Infra Projects Private Limited for Rs 44.67 crore.

InterGlobe Aviation flat at Rs 5,268.50 on the NSE. IndiGo and CFM signed an MoU that could lead to a record order for more than 1,000 LEAP-1A engines

At 11.03 am, Sensex traded 212.51 pts or 0.27% lower at 77,496.01; and Nifty 50 dipped 46.05 pts or 0.19% to 24,192.45.

CRISIL Ltd said the proposed closure of its wholly-owned subsidiary, Coalition Development Singapore Pte. Ltd. (Coalition Singapore), has been delayed as certain regulatory approvals in Singapore are still awaited. The closure, originally expected by July 21, 2026, is now expected to be completed within the next 12 months, by July 20, 2027, subject to the receipt of necessary statutory approvals. The subsidiary is no longer operational and has no ongoing business activities.

Shares of One 97 Communications, which operates Paytm, rose 2.5 per cent before giving up early gains on Tuesday after a sharp rise in consolidated net profit for the quarter ended June 2026. The company deferred its bonus proposal.

Citi has a buy rating on Paytm and raised its target raised its target price to ₹1,560

Shares of Advit Jewels Ltd fell 6.06 per cent to ₹200.58 on Tuesday, July 21, 2026, even as the Jaipur-based jewellery maker reported robust financial results for FY26, with net profit rising 35.56 per cent year-on-year to ₹34.39 crore.

The stock opened at ₹205 and touched a low of ₹199.10 during the session, against a previous close of ₹213.51. Total traded volume stood at 9.44 lakh shares, with a traded value of ₹19.36 crore. The company’s total market capitalisation was ₹918.86 crore at the time of reporting.

Advit Jewels shares drop 6% despite a 36% rise in net profit and strong FY26 financial results.

Supreme Infrastructure India said it has achieved the final financial milestone for implementing its Scheme of Compromise and Arrangement with lenders. With support from the promoter group, the company has created a ₹120-crore fixed deposit with lead bank State Bank of India, sufficient to meet the balance settlement obligations to the remaining lenders under the scheme.

Shares jumped over 8% to Rs 85.64 on the NSE.

CSM Technologies’ standalone net profit for the quarter ended March 2026 stood at Rs 8.38 crore, compared to Rs 5.80 crore in the same quarter last year.

In FY26, the PAY stood at Rs 20.25 crore, as against Rs 16.37 crore in the year-ago period.

The board has recommended a final dividend of Rs 0.50.

Shares gained 2% to Rs 101.22 on the NSE.

“It’s a good week for India’s asset management industry. What began with the AMC listings of 2017–18 continued this week — India’s largest mutual fund houses, managing more than half of the industry’s ₹82 lakh crore AUM, are now all listed on the public markets
